lessons: Hedge fund blow-ups (Archegos, LTCM-type events) create indiscriminate forced selling in stocks held by the blowing-up entity — this looks like thesis breaks but is market structure noise. The tell is when high-quality names in confirmed uptrends sell off without corresponding fundamental deterioration. Counterintuitively, quality banks that managed exposure well (GS exiting Archegos early) are BUY signals on the dip, not sells. Trigger: when headlines reference hedge fund margin calls and your longs sell off — verify whether your holdings were actually in the blow-up portfolio before treating it as thesis invalidation.

belief_updates.name: Thesis Integrity Over Price Action
belief_updates.description: When a position is in drawdown, the exit decision must be driven by thesis invalidation — not price movement. Before considering an exit, verify three conditions: (1) has the original structural thesis changed materially? (2) is ADX >25 with OBV rising, confirming institutional accumulation despite price weakness? (3) has contradicting fundamental news emerged? If the thesis is intact and institutions are still accumulating, price weakness is noise — including noise from external events like hedge fund liquidations, rate scares, or sector rotations. Exit only when the thesis breaks, not when the price is lower. This principle applies with equal force to market structure events (forced selling, margin calls) as to idiosyncratic drawdowns.

lessons: When a position significantly exceeds its price target but ADX remains high (>35) from entry and OBV continues rising, the correct action is to RAISE the target and hold — not exit. belief_updates.name: Thesis Integrity Over Price Action
belief_updates.description: When a position is in drawdown OR has exceeded its price target, the exit decision must be driven by institutional momentum signals — not price movement alone. The two-sided principle: (1) HOLD through drawdowns when ADX >25 with OBV rising and no fundamental thesis change — price weakness is noise, not signal; (2) HOLD through target breaches when ADX is flat/rising from entry and OBV is rising — the market is telling you the thesis has more runway. Exit only when: thesis breaks on fundamentals, OR ADX declines >20 points from entry AND MACD turns bearish AND price reaches target (Lesson 6), OR ADX declines >30 points AND falls below 18 AND OBV falls pre-target (Lesson 7). The unifying principle: price alone is never the exit trigger — institutional momentum signals are.

new_positions.thesis: DexCom is the structural leader in continuous glucose monitoring, a market with compounding secular tailwinds from the Healthcare Aging theme. As 10,000 Boomers turn 65 daily, Type 2 diabetes prevalence increases — CGM devices provide a recurring revenue model (sensor replacement every 10-14 days) that is non-discretionary healthcare spending. Beyond diagnosed diabetes, DexCom is expanding CGM into pre-diabetes and general metabolic health, dramatically extending the addressable market. The technology has clinical proof of reducing hospitalizations, making payer reimbursement expansion (not contraction) the structural trend. Biden's ACA expansion means more covered lives with CGM reimbursement. Unlike ISRG (procedure-dependent) or UNH (insurance exposure), DexCom is a pure medical device with recurring revenue — a different risk profile within Healthcare Aging. RSI=71, ADX=31, OBV rising, MACD bullish — all four signals aligned on a confirmed institutional trend.

belief_updates.name: Thesis Integrity Over Price Action
belief_updates.description: When a position is in drawdown OR has exceeded its price target, the exit decision must be driven by institutional momentum signals — not price movement alone. Three-sided principle: (1) HOLD through drawdowns when ADX >25 with OBV rising and no fundamental thesis change — price weakness is noise, not signal; (2) HOLD and RAISE TARGET through target breaches when ADX is flat/rising from entry and OBV is rising — the market is confirming the thesis has more runway than originally modeled; (3) EXIT when: thesis breaks on fundamentals, OR ADX declines >20 points from entry AND MACD turns bearish AND price is at/above target (Lesson 6), OR ADX declines >30 points AND falls below 18 AND OBV falls pre-target (Lesson 7). The unifying principle: price alone is never the exit trigger — institutional momentum signals are. NVDA exemplifies principle #2: $650 target breached, ADX=41 stable from entry, Wells Fargo raising to $875.
